event: Diagnosed with decompensated cirrhosis, abdominal infection, spontaneous peritonitis, hypoproteinemia, ARDS, and septic shock
Developed oral and perioral herpes infection
Large wound developed around the patient’s lips and in her mouth
No ongoing abdominal pain or distension
Perioral wound progressed to exhibit local bleeding with crusting of blood
Suggested treatment with oral antiviral drugs, intramuscular injection of nutritious nerve drugs, and application of topical penciclovir and mupirocin around the lips
Suggested wet application of topical nitrocilin around the lips
Diagnosed with perioral herpes infection secondary to noninvasive ventilation
Treated with oral and topical antiviral and antibiotic administration
Treated with nutritional and symptomatic supportive care
Treated with anti-inflammatory effect of furacilin
Treated with hemostasis with local epinephrine injection and application of topical thrombin lyophilized powder
